cataloging_subject.label: English Prose Works of Richard Rolle: a Selection from the Edition by Carl Horstmann
cataloging_subject.scope_note: A 2003 University of Michigan/CME electronic selection from volume 1 of Carl Horstmann's 1895 Yorkshire Writers edition. It contains eighteen count-once medieval works in fourteen DIV2 containers, including authentic Rolle works, disputed attributions, anonymous translations of Rolle's Latin works, and a Walter Hilton excerpt. The historical collection title must not become blanket authorship metadata.

catalog_summary.title: English Prose Works of Richard Rolle: a Selection from the Edition by Carl Horstmann
catalog_summary.author: Mixed: Richard Rolle, Walter Hilton, and anonymous or disputed authors
catalog_summary.date: Chiefly fourteenth-century works and later medieval adaptations; immediate edition 1895; electronic selection 2003
catalog_summary.region: England; authentic Rolle works are associated with northern England and Yorkshire, while witness strata include West Midlands and other English regions

date_statements.display: Chiefly fourteenth-century works and later medieval adaptations; exact dates are item-specific and often uncertain

notes: This broad selection-level statement does not turn witness copying regions into places of composition.

scope.description: Authentic Rolle works, translations of Rolle Latin material, and northern witness associations.

form_statements.description: Repository discovery form: prose. Two explicit L lines close p17; p02 contains verse-like passages encoded within P.

summaries.value: An edition-defined U-M/CME selection of eighteen Middle English works from Horstmann's 1895 Yorkshire Writers volume 1, preserving accepted Rolle works alongside disputed attributions, a Walter Hilton excerpt, anonymous adaptations, and English translations/compilations over Rolle Latin sources.
Three DIV1 editorial sections contain fourteen DIV2 containers, eighteen count-once works, twenty-one heterogeneous DIV3 nodes, thirty-one DIV4 nodes, and twenty-eight primary source streams.

extent.description: Eighteen count-once works in three editorial groups and fourteen DIV2 containers. All use the repository's prose structural form. The two explicit XML L elements are a closing couplet in p17's Thornton branch. Allen also identifies a ten-line alliterative passage and two lyrics in p02, but CME encodes them inside P, so structural L metrics undercount verse-like material.

content_parts.label: Canonical Works
content_parts.summary: Editorial group containing 5 DIV2 containers and 9 count-once works.

content_parts.label: The Form of Living
content_parts.summary: Richard Rolle; prose; Horstmann vol. 1 pp. 3-49.

locators.value: part III ff. 1r-22v; Horstmann's continuous codex numbering ff. 101r-122v; XML branch rollewks:1.1.1; role parallel_base_transcription
ff. 40r-57r; XML branch rollewks:1.1.2; role parallel_base_transcription

notes: Harley 1022 ff. 47r-61v supplies the first portion and collations across vol. 1 pp. 3-49 but is not an independently encoded U-M witness stream.
Responsibility: Richard Rolle
Qualified date: probably 1348-1349, written for the anchoress Margaret Kirkeby
Region/witness association: northern England; associated with Yorkshire; not promoted to blanket composition place.

content_parts.label: Ego Dormio et Cor Meum Vigilat
content_parts.summary: Richard Rolle; prose; Horstmann vol. 1 pp. 50-61.

locators.value: part III ff. 22v-29r; Horstmann's continuous codex numbering ff. 122v-129r; XML branch rollewks:1.2.1; role parallel_base_transcription
ff. 77r-81r; one missing passage supplied from Vernon; XML branch rollewks:1.2.2; role parallel_base_transcription_with_lacuna; excludes rollewks:1.2.2.3/ADD[1]
current f. 338vb, within Ego Dormio ff. 338ra-339ra; Allen's legacy foliation calls the work ff. 369-370; XML branch rollewks:1.2.2.3/ADD[1]; role inserted_supply

notes: The Rawlinson branch locator explicitly excludes its nested ADD so the two modeled source paths do not overlap semantically. The supplied ADD passage is wholly within current f. 338vb, Digital Bodleian canvas 563, and then returns to Rawlinson A 389.

Allen identifies a ten-line alliterative unrhymed passage and two irregular-rhyme lyrics in Ego Dormio, including Cantus amoris on printed pp. 60-61. CME encodes these within P rather than L, so the repository's structural form remains prose while this embedded verse-like material is explicitly noted.
Responsibility: Richard Rolle
Qualified date: fourteenth century; Allen tentatively places it around 1343 and considerably before 1349
Region/witness association: northern England; not promoted to blanket composition place.

content_parts.label: The Abbey of the Holy Ghost
content_parts.summary: anonymous Middle English adapter of the French Sainte Abbaye; Rolle authorship possible but unproved and widely anonymous; prose; Horstmann vol. 1 pp. 321-337.

locators.value: ff. 271-276; exact side boundaries not overclaimed; XML branch rollewks:2.7; role base_transcription

notes: Thornton remains the sole primary transcription stream. Horstmann's apparatus additionally collates Laud Misc. 210, Vernon, Harley 2406, and Harley 1704 for selected readings; those are supporting relationships only.

Lambeth MS 432 preserves an attribution witness in a combined Abbey/Charter catalog item beginning f. 37v; the exact Abbey/Charter boundary is unresolved, and the next catalog item begins f. 68v (catalog f. 68b). The Charter is excluded from the rollewks selection.
Responsibility: anonymous Middle English adapter of the French Sainte Abbaye; Rolle authorship possible but unproved and widely anonymous
Qualified date: late medieval adaptation
Region/witness association: England; northern Thornton witness and a fifteenth-century Lambeth attribution witness; not promoted to blanket composition place.

open_questions.question: How does U-M intend the current public-domain statement to interact with the restrictive legacy availability text embedded in the pinned XML for redistribution of the encoding?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for written clarification before redistributing their XML as a provider package.

open_questions.question: Do complete pre-existing color surrogates exist for Dd.5.64, Ll.1.8, or Add.3042, and what would whole-codex delivery and reuse cost?
next_steps: Use CUL's online form and distinguish private-study supply from publication/commercial permissions.

open_questions.question: Will Lincoln supply/license a complete MS 91 surrogate, or is the revised 1977 Scolar facsimile the practical complete reproduction route?
next_steps: Ask Lincoln Cathedral Library and record whole-object coverage and reuse terms.

open_questions.question: Can complete manuscript images establish exact recto/verso endpoints for Rawlinson C 285 ff. 64-68 and the Rawlinson A 389 Vernon-supply join?
next_steps: Request whole-codex or exact-folio images from Bodleian while retaining the full-manuscript preference.

open_questions.question: Can direct image inspection reconcile Horstmann's Arundel 507 misbinding sequence with current foliation and exact text sides?
next_steps: Request or inspect a complete Arundel 507 surrogate before normalizing loci.

open_questions.question: Will Lambeth supply and license a complete color surrogate of MS 432 despite the ordinary forty-scan limit?
next_steps: Email archives@churchofengland.org and specify bindings, flyleaves, blanks, all folios, the combined Abbey/Charter item beginning f. 37v with its unresolved internal boundary before the next item at f. 68v (catalog f. 68b), and intended repository/POD uses.

notes: The historical title is not blanket Rolle authorship.
Witness dates and regions remain separate from work composition claims.
All source access, local-cache, facsimile, and rights detail is in the linked lineage manifest.
